name: Ink (React for CLIs)

performance.notes: Terminal rendering via yoga-layout; negligible overhead for typical UIs

agent_readiness.notes: Build interactive CLIs using React components. render(<App />) renders to terminal. Components: Box (flexbox layout via yoga-layout), Text (colors/styles), Newline, Spacer, Static (non-re-rendered output). Hooks: useInput, useApp, useFocus, useStdout. useState/useEffect work normally. Pastel is Ink's styled-system alternative. Used by Gatsby CLI, Prisma, Shopify CLI, create-next-app. TypeScript types. v5 requires React 18. Full flexbox layout system. Alternative to raw Blessed/terminal-kit.

gotchas: Requires React familiarity — overkill for simple CLIs
Non-interactive environments (CI) may not render correctly
best_when: Complex interactive CLIs with stateful UI (spinners, tables, forms)
Teams already using React who want familiar patterns for CLIs
CLIs needing real-time updating output (dashboards, watchers)
Projects using Pastel framework for styled CLI components
avoid_when: Simple CLIs (Chalk + Commander simpler and faster)
CI/CD scripts that output to non-TTY
Teams unfamiliar with React wanting minimal dependencies
summary: Ink lets you build interactive CLIs using React components. render(<App />) renders to terminal with Box (flexbox), Text, and hooks (useInput, useApp). Used by Gatsby CLI, Prisma, create-next-app. Full flexbox layout via yoga-layout. TypeScript types. Best for complex stateful CLIs where React component model adds value — simpler tools should use Chalk/Commander directly.
